steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is Tannen’s claim that noncompetitive formats with multiple perspectives are “more informative than the presentation of multiple perspectives in a noncompetitive format.” This directly links to the study’s goal of comparing which news format increases participants’ understanding.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice C aligns with Tannen’s hypothesis by showing that the panel format (noncompetitive, multiperspective) leads to participants answering more questions correctly than the debate or single commentator. A and B focus on perceived knowledge, which Tannen doesn’t prioritize. D compares a single commentator to a debate, ignoring the panel altogether. That matches choice C — C is correct.
